clusters.title: Russia launches deadliest aerial assault of the year on Ukraine
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia launches deadliest aerial assault of the year on Ukraine
bullet_summary: Nearly 700 drones and 19 missiles struck cities across the country overnight, killing 16 people, officials said
A massive Russian drone and missile barrage killed at least 16 people in cities across Ukraine, more than four years into the war with peace talks stalled, Ukrainian officials said on Thursday
The Ukrainian air force said Russia had launched 659 drones in the attack and 44 missiles, over the last 24 hours, adding that its air defence units downed 636 drones and 31 missiles
Russia launched overnight drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, killing three people, including a child, and triggering air alerts in Kyiv, officials said Thursday

items.title: Russia launches deadliest aerial assault of the year on Ukraine
items.standfirst: Nearly 700 drones and 19 missiles struck cities across the country overnight, killing 16 people, officials said. Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, was among the hardest hit.
items.content: Nearly 700 drones and 19 missiles struck cities across the country overnight, killing 16 people, officials said. Ukraine's capital, Kyiv, was among the hardest hit.

items.title: Russian massive strikes kill at least 16 across Ukraine
items.standfirst: A massive Russian drone and missile barrage killed at least 16 people in cities across Ukraine, more than four years into the war with peace talks stalled, Ukrainian officials said on Thursday. The Ukrainian air force said Russia had launched 659 drones in the attack and 44 missiles, over the last 24 hours, adding that its air defence units downed 636 drones and 31 missiles. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze reports from Kyiv, Ukraine.
items.content: A massive Russian drone and missile barrage killed at least 16 people in cities across Ukraine, more than four years into the war with peace talks stalled, Ukrainian officials said on Thursday. The Ukrainian air force said Russia had launched 659 drones in the attack and 44 missiles, over the last 24 hours, adding that its air defence units downed 636 drones and 31 missiles. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze reports from Kyiv, Ukraine.

items.title: Russian missiles and drones bombard Ukraine for hours, killing at least 18 people
items.standfirst: Russia unleashed a barrage of hundreds of drones and missiles against Ukraine overnight, killing at least 18 people in its most intense attack of the year.
items.content: Russia unleashed a barrage of hundreds of drones and missiles against Ukraine overnight, killing at least 18 people in its most intense attack of the year.

items.title: Wave of Russian drone and missile attacks kill at least 16 in Ukraine
items.standfirst: Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone attack killed two children in Russia, officials say.
items.content: Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone attack killed two children in Russia, officials say.

items.title: Russian missiles and drones bombard Ukraine in hourslong attack
items.standfirst: Russia hammered civilian areas of Ukraine with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in an attack that stretched for hours killing at least 16 people.
items.content: Russia hammered civilian areas of Ukraine with hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in an attack that stretched for hours killing at least 16 people.(Image credit: Evgeniy Maloletka)

items.title: Russian strikes kill three, including child, as drones and missiles hit Kyiv
items.standfirst: Russia launched overnight drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, killing three people, including a child, and triggering air alerts in Kyiv, officials said Thursday. Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said a 12-year-old boy and a 35-year-old woman were among the dead as strikes intensified after months of near-daily bombardments.
items.content: Russia launched overnight drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, killing three people, including a child, and triggering air alerts in Kyiv, officials said Thursday. Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said a 12-year-old boy and a 35-year-old woman were among the dead as strikes intensified after months of near-daily bombardments.

items.title: South African politician Julius Malema given five-year jail term for gun offence
items.standfirst: Leader of leftwing Economic Freedom Fighters was convicted last year for firing rifle in the air at 2018 rallyThe South African leftwing politician Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for firing a rifle in the air at a political rally in 2018.Lawyers for the leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters, South Africa’s fourth largest political party, immediately sought leave to appeal. The magistrate is currently considering whether to grant this. Continue reading...

The magistrate is currently considering whether to grant this. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Turkish police detain 162 people over online praise for school shootings
clusters.neutral_headline: Turkish police detain 162 people over online praise for school shootings
bullet_summary: A student has killed nine people and wounded 13 others in Turkey's second school shooting in two days, officials said
Reported by BBC World, France24, NBC World and others

items.title: Turkish police detain 162 people over online praise for school shootings
items.standfirst: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.
items.content: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

items.title: Teenager kills nine, wounds 13 in Turkey school shooting
items.standfirst: A 14-year-old armed with five guns opened fire at a Turkish school on Wednesday, killing nine people, wounding 13 and sparking scenes of mayhem as students jumped out of windows to escape. The attack in the southern province of Kahramanmaras province was Turkey's second such incident in as many days, shocking a country where school shootings are a rare occurrence.
items.content: A 14-year-old armed with five guns opened fire at a Turkish school on Wednesday, killing nine people, wounding 13 and sparking scenes of mayhem as students jumped out of windows to escape. The attack in the southern province of Kahramanmaras province was Turkey's second such incident in as many days, shocking a country where school shootings are a rare occurrence.

items.title: Turkey orders mass arrests over online praise for recent school shootings
items.standfirst: Turkish police have ordered the arrest of scores of people accused of either praising or spreading fake news about the country's recent school shootings online. At least nine people died in a school shooting in the southern province of Kahramanmaras on Wednesday, and on Tuesday, a former student opened fire at a high school in the southeastern district of Siverek, injuring 16.
items.content: Turkish police have ordered the arrest of scores of people accused of either praising or spreading fake news about the country's recent school shootings online. At least nine people died in a school shooting in the southern province of Kahramanmaras on Wednesday, and on Tuesday, a former student opened fire at a high school in the southeastern district of Siverek, injuring 16.

items.title: Student kills at least nine people in Turkey's second school shooting in two days
items.standfirst: A 14-year-old student fatally shot at least nine people, including eight of his fellow students, and wounded at least 13 others at a middle school in southeastern Turkey. This is the country’s second school shooting in two days. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports on Turkey’s deadliest school shooting.
items.content: A 14-year-old student fatally shot at least nine people, including eight of his fellow students, and wounded at least 13 others at a middle school in southeastern Turkey. This is the country’s second school shooting in two days. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports on Turkey’s deadliest school shooting.

items.title: Student kills nine people in Turkey's second school shooting in two days
items.standfirst: A student has killed nine people and wounded 13 others in Turkey's second school shooting in two days, officials said.
items.content: A student has killed nine people and wounded 13 others in Turkey's second school shooting in two days, officials said.

bullet_summary: Reported by Financial Times, France24, Guardian World

items.title: Russia hits Ukraine with largest air attack this year
items.standfirst: Missiles and drones killed and injured residents in Kyiv, Odesa, Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia
items.content: Missiles and drones killed and injured residents in Kyiv, Odesa, Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia

items.title: Ukraine: At least 16 killed in overnight Russian strikes
items.standfirst: 🔴 🇷🇺 🇺🇦 At least 16 people were killed in #Russian strikes across #Ukraine overnight between Wednesday and Thursday, which targeted residential buildings in the capital #Kyiv as well as in the cities of #Dnipro and #Odesa.
items.content: 🔴 🇷🇺 🇺🇦 At least 16 people were killed in #Russian strikes across #Ukraine overnight between Wednesday and Thursday, which targeted residential buildings in the capital #Kyiv as well as in the cities of #Dnipro and #Odesa.

items.title: More than a dozen killed in Russian attacks on Kyiv, other Ukrainian cities
items.standfirst: More than a dozen people, including at least one child, have been killed in Russian missile and drone attacks on infrastructure and residential buildings in Kyiv and in other Ukrainian cities like Odesa and Dnipro overnight to Thursday. Several dozens of civilians were also injured in the assaults.
items.content: More than a dozen people, including at least one child, have been killed in Russian missile and drone attacks on infrastructure and residential buildings in Kyiv and in other Ukrainian cities like Odesa and Dnipro overnight to Thursday. Several dozens of civilians were also injured in the assaults.

items.title: Russia pummels Ukraine with drone and missile strikes, killing at least 12
items.standfirst: Missile and drone attacks on the port city of Odesa killed six people, with other fatalities recorded in Kyiv and DniproRussian strikes killed at least 12 people in Ukraine, local authorities have said, after Moscow pummelled its neighbour in overnight attacks.Missile and drone attacks on the southern port city of Odesa killed six people, the head of the city’s military administration, Sergiy Lysak, wrote on Telegram on Thursday. Continue reading...
items.content: Missile and drone attacks on the port city of Odesa killed six people, with other fatalities recorded in Kyiv and DniproRussian strikes killed at least 12 people in Ukraine, local authorities have said, after Moscow pummelled its neighbour in overnight attacks.Missile and drone attacks on the southern port city of Odesa killed six people, the head of the city’s military administration, Sergiy Lysak, wrote on Telegram on Thursday. clusters.title: Starmer tells social media firms in No 10 meeting ‘things can’t go on like this’
clusters.neutral_headline: Starmer tells social media firms in No 10 meeting ‘things can’t go on like this’
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories, Bloomberg Technology

items.title: Starmer tells social media firms in No 10 meeting ‘things can’t go on like this’
items.standfirst: PM summons senior figures from Meta, TikTok, Google and X and says social media is ‘putting our children at risk’Keir Starmer has told social media bosses “things can’t go on like this” in a Downing Street meeting over internet safety.The prime minister summoned senior figures at Meta, TikTok, Google and X to No 10 on Thursday morning as his government considers imposing new restrictions on platforms, including an Australia-style ban for under-16s. Meta owns Facebook and Instagram, and Google owns YouTube. Continue reading...
items.content: PM summons senior figures from Meta, TikTok, Google and X and says social media is ‘putting our children at risk’Keir Starmer has told social media bosses “things can’t go on like this” in a Downing Street meeting over internet safety.The prime minister summoned senior figures at Meta, TikTok, Google and X to No 10 on Thursday morning as his government considers imposing new restrictions on platforms, including an Australia-style ban for under-16s. Meta owns Facebook and Instagram, and Google owns YouTube. Continue reading...

items.title: Things can't go on like this with online safety, Starmer tells tech bosses
items.standfirst: It comes as the government continues to consult on whether to ban under-16s from social media in the UK.
items.content: It comes as the government continues to consult on whether to ban under-16s from social media in the UK.

items.title: Starmer Tells Social Media Firms Online Safety Change Is Needed
items.standfirst: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer told representatives from the largest US tech companies that they “can’t go on like this” when it comes to online protections for children.
items.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer told representatives from the largest US tech companies that they “can’t go on like this” when it comes to online protections for children.

clusters.title: Netanyahu will speak with Lebanese president Aoun, Israeli minister says
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump says Israel and Lebanon’s leaders will speak on Thursday
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump had said the "leaders" of Israel and Lebanon would speak for the first time on Thursday, in what would be a historic first
The meeting comes as President Donald Trump announced the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak later on Thursday about halting the fighting between them
US President Donald Trump said the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak on Thursday in what would be a historic first, but there was no confirmation from either side

items.title: Netanyahu will speak with Lebanese president Aoun, Israeli minister says
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will speak with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun on Thursday, according to Israel's Minister for Innovation, Science and Technology Gila Gamliel. US President Donald Trump had said the "leaders" of Israel and Lebanon would speak for the first time on Thursday, in what would be a historic first. Gamliel's statement was the first confirmation from an official Israeli source that talks would take place. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will speak with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun on Thursday, according to Israel's Minister for Innovation, Science and Technology Gila Gamliel. US President Donald Trump had said the "leaders" of Israel and Lebanon would speak for the first time on Thursday, in what would be a historic first. Gamliel's statement was the first confirmation from an official Israeli source that talks would take place. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: Pakistani army chief visits Tehran in hopes for renewed talks between US and Iran
items.standfirst: Pakistan’s army chief is set to meet with Iranian officials in Tehran on Thursday in a bid to extend the ceasefire which paused almost seven weeks of war between Israel, the US and Iran that have killed thousands of people and upended global markets by disrupting the flow of oil. The meeting comes as President Donald Trump announced the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak later on Thursday about halting the fighting between them.
items.content: Pakistan’s army chief is set to meet with Iranian officials in Tehran on Thursday in a bid to extend the ceasefire which paused almost seven weeks of war between Israel, the US and Iran that have killed thousands of people and upended global markets by disrupting the flow of oil. The meeting comes as President Donald Trump announced the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak later on Thursday about halting the fighting between them.

items.title: Trump says Lebanese and Israeli leaders to speak
items.standfirst: US President Donald ​Trump said Lebanese and Israeli leaders will speak for the first time in decades on Thursday, while Pakistan said peace in Lebanon was essential for talks it is mediating between Washington and Tehran on ending the Iran war. The Lebanon conflict spiralled out of the US-Israeli war with Iran, with the Iran-backed Hezbollah opening fire in support of ​Tehran on March 2, ‌prompting an Israeli offensive in Lebanon just 15 months after the last major conflict.
items.content: US President Donald ​Trump said Lebanese and Israeli leaders will speak for the first time in decades on Thursday, while Pakistan said peace in Lebanon was essential for talks it is mediating between Washington and Tehran on ending the Iran war. The Lebanon conflict spiralled out of the US-Israeli war with Iran, with the Iran-backed Hezbollah opening fire in support of ​Tehran on March 2, ‌prompting an Israeli offensive in Lebanon just 15 months after the last major conflict.

items.title: Lebanon expresses cautious optimism on US president's claim of direct talks with Israel
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak on Thursday in what would be a historic first, but there was no confirmation from either side. FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen reports from Beirut, Lebanon.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak on Thursday in what would be a historic first, but there was no confirmation from either side. FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen reports from Beirut, Lebanon.

items.title: 'Confusion' as Trump says Israel, Lebanon leaders to hold talks
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak on Thursday in what would be a historic first, but there was no confirmation from either side. Lebanon was pulled into the Middle East war on March 2 after the Lebanon-based armed group Hezbollah, which is backed by Iran, attacked Israel. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Philip Turle tells us more.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said the leaders of Israel and Lebanon will speak on Thursday in what would be a historic first, but there was no confirmation from either side. Lebanon was pulled into the Middle East war on March 2 after the Lebanon-based armed group Hezbollah, which is backed by Iran, attacked Israel. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Philip Turle tells us more.

items.title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon’s leaders will speak on Thursday
items.standfirst: No official response from Israel or Lebanon to US statement, which comes after Netanyahu ordered expansion of invasion.
items.content: No official response from Israel or Lebanon to US statement, which comes after Netanyahu ordered expansion of invasion.

clusters.neutral_headline: France seeks release of 86-year-old French widow detained by ICE
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, Guardian World

items.title: French government seeking release of 86-year-old French widow detained by ICE
items.standfirst: The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.
items.content: The French government is pressing the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody in Louisiana after she was detained earlier this month.(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)

items.title: France seeks release of 86-year-old French widow detained by ICE
items.standfirst: Agents detained Marie-Therese Ross in Alabama on 1 April after she overstayed her 90-day visa, according to DHSThe French government is pressing the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody after she was detained earlier this month.US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents detained Marie-Therese Ross in Alabama on 1 April after she overstayed her 90-day visa, according to DHS. Ross is now being held at a federal immigration detention facility in Louisiana. Continue reading...
items.content: Agents detained Marie-Therese Ross in Alabama on 1 April after she overstayed her 90-day visa, according to DHSThe French government is pressing the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to release the 86-year-old French widow of a military veteran from immigration custody after she was detained earlier this month.US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents detained Marie-Therese Ross in Alabama on 1 April after she overstayed her 90-day visa, according to DHS. Ross is now being held at a federal immigration detention facility in Louisiana. Continue reading...

clusters.title: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
clusters.neutral_headline: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
items.standfirst: Appointment of Roelf Meyer seen as attempt to improve relations amid false US accusations of ‘white genocide’South Africa has appointed a former apartheid government chief negotiator during the talks that ended white rule in the 1990s as ambassador to the US, in what is seen as an attempt to improve the deeply strained diplomatic relationship between the two countries.Roelf Meyer replaces Ebrahim Rasool, who was expelled in March 2025 after he criticised the Trump administration. Continue reading...
items.content: Appointment of Roelf Meyer seen as attempt to improve relations amid false US accusations of ‘white genocide’South Africa has appointed a former apartheid government chief negotiator during the talks that ended white rule in the 1990s as ambassador to the US, in what is seen as an attempt to improve the deeply strained diplomatic relationship between the two countries.Roelf Meyer replaces Ebrahim Rasool, who was expelled in March 2025 after he criticised the Trump administration. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Roelf Meyer played a key role as a negotiator during talks to end white-minority rule in South Africa.
